data.narrative:
Atom Bodies is a Series A biotech company founded by Tyler Hayes, Doug Satzger, and Eric Monsef, having raised $2M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies—8 private and 3 public—in the Biotech & Health sector at Series A and B stages, including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression anchors to the $2M total raised as a baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $4M. Peer-median triangulation examines six same-sector comparables at identical or adjacent funding stages, with valuations ranging from $100M to $3.3B and a median of $805M. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Atom Bodies' revenue is not disclosed; the sector median P/S multiple across 3 public tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) is 7.4x but remains unusable without revenue data.

The triangulated valuation range is $3M to $926M, with a median of $404M across the two applicable methods. The wide dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty around stage-appropriate valuation for an early-stage biotech relative to later-stage peers. The 2.0x progression multiple offers a floor anchored to recent funding; the peer median represents mid-market positioning but should be weighted cautiously given the heterogeneity of biotech valuations and Atom Bodies' earlier stage relative to the Series B comparison set. Revenue trajectory and clinical/technical differentiation remain critical but unquantified drivers of ultimate valuation.
